skill.name: comprehensive-review-pr-enhance
skill.description: Create clear pull request descriptions, review checklists, risk notes, test summaries, and split recommendations for software changes.
skill.summary: Helps turn code changes into review-ready pull requests with structured context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are false positives from Markdown fences, placeholders, and Mermaid labels. The only confirmed risk factor is documentation that demonstrates local git subprocess commands for PR analysis; no network access, credential access, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Local Git Command Examples in Documentation
low_findings.description: The playbook includes Python examples that run local git diff commands with subprocess.run and a Bash split workflow. This confirms the external command pattern, but the commands are documentation examples for local repository analysis and do not use shell=True, networking, secrets, or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls and Bash commands are directly visible. Context shows local git-only PR analysis examples, so the security impact is low.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown and Placeholder False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick, weak crypto, C2 keyword, and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives. Inspected locations contain Markdown code fences, PR template placeholders, a Mermaid node label C2[Database], or normal skill role text rather than malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were inspected and do not contain weak cryptography, reconnaissance, C2 behavior, or Ruby execution. They are Markdown content and PR templates.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python subprocess.run for Local Git Inspection
dangerous_patterns.description: The playbook shows subprocess.run used to execute git diff commands for changed-file and change-stat analysis. This is a command execution pattern, but it is limited to local git inspection in sample code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact subprocess.run calls are present. The commands are local git diff commands and no shell or network use is evident.

content.value_statement: Large or unclear pull requests slow review and hide risk. This skill creates structured summaries, test notes, risk sections, and reviewer guidance.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes changed files, scope, intent, and review impact.
Drafts pull request descriptions with tests, risks, and rollout notes.
Creates context-aware review checklists for code, tests, docs, and configuration.
Identifies large pull requests and suggests logical split plans.
Formats reviewer response templates for common feedback situations.
Adds optional coverage, architecture, and risk sections when context supports them.
limitations: Requires a diff, change list, or clear change summary from the user.
Does not replace a full security or correctness review.
Cannot verify test results unless logs or command output are provided.
Some playbook examples require local git context if copied into automation.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature PR
use_cases.description: Turn a feature branch diff into a clear description with scope, motivation, tests, and reviewer notes.

use_cases.title: Review a Large Change
use_cases.description: Summarize risk, highlight files that need close review, and suggest smaller follow-up pull requests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team PRs
use_cases.description: Create repeatable PR templates and checklists that make reviews easier across a team.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PR Description
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: the user has a short change summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request description from this change summary. Include summary, what changed, tests, risks, and reviewer notes: [paste summary].

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: the user has a draft PR body.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this PR description for clarity and reviewability. Keep the intent, add missing test and risk sections, and remove vague wording: [paste draft].

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Diff for Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: the user can provide a diff or file list.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this diff or changed-file list. Summarize scope, affected areas, testing needs, likely risks, and focused reviewer guidance: [paste diff or list].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Large PR Split
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: the user has a large or mixed change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this large change and propose a practical PR split plan. Group files by logical purpose and include dependency order, risks, and review strategy: [paste context].

output_examples.input: A backend change adds account export endpoints, updates permissions, and adds unit tests.
output_examples.output: A PR description with summary, changed areas, permission review notes, test evidence, rollout risk, and suggested reviewers.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes 45 files across UI, API, and database migrations.
output_examples.output: A split recommendation that separates database work, API changes, UI updates, and follow-up cleanup into reviewable parts.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks why a simpler implementation was not used.
output_examples.output: A concise response that acknowledges the question, explains tradeoffs, lists alternatives, and proposes a next step.

best_practices: Provide the diff, changed-file list, test output, and linked issue when available.
Ask for explicit risk, rollout, and reviewer sections before requesting final wording.
Use the generated checklist as a starting point and remove items that do not apply.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent test results or approval status.
Do not use it as a substitute for reviewing the code itself.
Do not merge generated PR sections without checking project conventions.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete pull request description?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft a complete description when you provide a diff, file list, or clear change summary.

faq.question: Does it run tests or verify builds?
faq.answer: No. It can summarize provided test output, but it cannot confirm results without evidence.

faq.question: Can it help split a large pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. It can group changes by purpose and suggest a practical sequence for smaller pull requests.

faq.question: Is this useful for code review findings?
faq.answer: It helps organize reviewer notes and responses, but it does not replace a detailed code review.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is there a low security risk rating?
faq.answer: The playbook includes local git command examples. They are documentation examples, but users should review them before automation.
